name: VIVOS INC
business: Vivos Inc. develops and sells yttrium-90 based precision radionuclide therapy devices for treating solid tumors in humans (RadioGel) and animals (IsoPet). It also sells PLGA-g-PEG polymers and PrecisionGel hydrogel.
namedCustomers: Johns Hopkins University Veterinary Clinical Trials Network
Animal Emergency & Specialty Center of Knoxville
University of Wisconsin School of Veterinary Medicine
University of Illinois College of Veterinary Medicine
University of Florida Large Animal Hospital
University of Florida Small Animal Hospital

exposures.sentence: Vivos Inc. is a radiation oncology medical device company engaged in the development of its yttrium-90 ( Y-90 ) based precision radionuclide therapy devices.

catalysts.event: IDE resubmission targeted by end of Q1 or April 2026
catalysts.sentence: Currently, the IDE is in preparation under this expert's guidance. The Company is positioned to incorporate final FDA input and submit the complete IDE application in the near term (targeted by the end of the first quarter or in April 2026).

catalysts.event: 2026 Regulation A+ Offering qualified by SEC
catalysts.sentence: In March 2026, the Company filed with the SEC an offering statement on Form 1-A ... which offering was qualified by the SEC as of March 5, 2026.

catalysts.event: Domestic production facility at APEL targeted operational in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company's strategic target is to establish one domestic production facility where Vivos will serve as the manufacturer of record and one international production facility, both operational in 2026.

catalysts.event: International production facility in India targeted operational by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The international facility is being pursued through ongoing discussions with experienced radiopharmaceutical contract manufacturers in India, aligned with the establishment and activities of Vivos Scientific India LLP (VSIL), to reduce shipping costs, enhance logistical efficiency, and expand access to global markets.

catalysts.event: IsoPet Animal Therapy Division targeting breakeven in 2026
catalysts.sentence: With significant growth achieved in 2025 including a reported 1,200% year-over-year increase in administered therapies and expansion of the certified clinic network the Animal Therapy Division (IsoPet ) is positioned to reach breakeven status in 2026, supported by ongoing profitability-focused initiatives, continued clinic expansion, and improved operational efficiency.

tamClaims.claim: The United States is home to approximately 163.6 million pet dogs and cats.
tamClaims.sentence: The United States is home to approximately 163.6 million pet dogs and cats (with recent 2025 estimates from the American Veterinary Medical Association (the AVMA ) indicating about 87.3 million dogs and 76.3 million cats).

tamClaims.claim: Roughly half of dogs over age 10 develop cancer.
tamClaims.sentence: roughly half of dogs over age 10 and a significant portion of older cats develop cancer, which is the number one natural cause of death in senior dogs and cats, accounting for nearly 50% of pet deaths in many cases.

tamClaims.claim: About 6 million dogs are diagnosed with cancer each year.
tamClaims.sentence: The National Cancer Institute and veterinary sources estimate that about 6 million dogs are diagnosed with cancer each year (more than 16,000 per day), with similar high incidence in cats.
